wiki.extract: The R. J. Reynolds Tobacco Company (RJR) is an American tobacco manufacturing company based in Winston-Salem, North Carolina. Founded by namesake R. J. Reynolds in 1875, it is the largest tobacco company in the United States. The company is a wholly owned subsidiary of Reynolds American, itself a wholly owned subsidiary of British American Tobacco.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy

sampleRecords.nature: Closed trauma involving internal organs, major blood vessels
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was loading snow guns and jacks onto a dump-style truck. The truck was parked on an incline and the door closed on the employee. The employee was hospitalized for contusions and a lacerated liver.

sampleRecords.event_description: Pedestrian struck by vehicle in roadway, unspecified
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was in the middle of a roundabout when they were struck by a vehicle, resulting in injuries to their head, neck, back, hips and legs.

sampleRecords.event_description: Injured by slipping or swinging object held by injured worker
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was chopping basil with a knife and severed the tip of his left thumb.

sampleRecords.event_description: Other fall to lower level 16 to 20 feet
sampleRecords.final_narrative: While descending a ladder from a roof, an employee slipped and fell 16 feet, requiring hospitalization.

sampleRecords.event_description: Struck against stationary object or equipment, n.e.c.
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was testing a slide ride at startup. The water was turned onto the slide, but had not yet filled the bottom pool when the employee tested it. The employee slid down the slide and, once at the bottom, slid across the shallow pool (1/4" of water) and into the end of the slide wall.
